title: Lycopodioides subarborescens
taxonomy: Lycopodioides subarborescens belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida and the phylum Lycopodiophyta. It is a type of club moss, a group of ancient, vascular plants. The class Lycopodiopsida is further divided into several orders, with Lycopodiales being one of the main orders. Lycopodioides subarborescens is native to tropical regions and is often found in humid, shaded areas. Its classification is consistent with that of other club mosses, indicating its evolutionary relationships.

intro: Lycopodioides subarborescens, also known as the climbing club moss, is a species of plant in the Lycopodiaceae family, characterized by its woody, climbing stems and small, scale-like leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lycopodioides subarborescens include lignans, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and bronchitis.
